reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes "possible ranges of a real polynomial f(x,y)". Using `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) ℝ` correctly represents bivariate polynomials with real coefficients, and `{z : ℝ | ∃ x : Fin 2 → ℝ, MvPolynomial.eval x f = z}` is exactly the range of f over ℝ². The outer set builder `{... | f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) ℝ}` correctly quantifies over all such polynomials. The claimed solution set (singletons, closed half-lines Ici/Iic, open half-lines Ioi/Iio, and univ) matches the standard answer — open half-lines appear because of examples like (xy-1)²+x² whose range is (0,∞), which distinguishes the bivariate case from univariate. There is no hidden type mismatch or hypothesis drift, and the quantifier structure matches the informal reading.
